Convert an R specs list to a Julia Dict{String,Any}

Source: R/run.R
dot-specs_to_julia.Rd

Sends the specs list to Julia through JuliaConnectoR::juliaCall() and lets the Julia helper Clade.r_specs_to_dict() unpack it into a Dict{String,Any}. This replaces the earlier string-interpolation approach which required manual escaping for every scalar type. JuliaConnectoR serialises an R named list as an RConnector.ElementList whose scalars retain their R types (integer, double, logical, character), and the Julia helper rebuilds the Dict keyed by string.

Usage

.specs_to_julia(specs)

Arguments

specs

A validated specs list from default_specs().

Value

A Julia proxy for the resulting Dict{String,Any}, suitable for passing to Clade.run_clade().

Details

Values that JuliaConnectoR cannot serialise – single NAs and zero-length character vectors – are dropped before sending. Julia reads optional fields via get(specs, key, default), so an absent key is equivalent to nothing or the coded default.

On this page

Developed by Shinichi Nakagawa.
